jokes.text: D'oh! Ah!
jokes.setup: Homer's classic opening couch gag reaction
jokes.punchline: The iconic 'D'oh!' catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase - economical, character-specific, and endlessly quotable

jokes.text: We're not worthy. We're not worthy.
jokes.setup: Cultural legends meeting Lisa
jokes.punchline: Wayne's World reference from prestigious figures
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference without much twist - serviceable but not inspired

jokes.text: Well, here's another low-rated annual event: spring-cleaning
jokes.setup: Lisa mentions the Kennedy Center Honors
jokes.punchline: Marge comparing spring cleaning to low-rated TV
jokes.explanation: Smart parallel that feels natural from Marge's practical perspective

jokes.text: You've gotta get rid of those Furbys. They've turned feral.
jokes.setup: Family cleaning out old toys
jokes.punchline: Furbys have 'turned feral' (sounds like 'furby' + 'feral')
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels - the pun and the visual of wild Furbys

jokes.text: [FURBYS GROWLING]
jokes.setup: Marge mentions feral Furbys
jokes.punchline: Sound effect of Furbys actually growling like wild animals
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-through on the feral Furby joke - economical sound gag

jokes.text: Ooh! Mad Libs.
jokes.setup: Lisa finds an interesting old book in Latin
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks ancient Latin text is Mad Libs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - his ignorance creates the humor, well-earned through character

jokes.text: Hey, Lis, think you're well-adjusted?
jokes.setup: Bart finds videotapes labeled 'Marge and Homer Get Dirty'
jokes.punchline: Implying Lisa might see something traumatic
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection setup that plays on audience expectations

jokes.text: Every tape is pumpkin carving
jokes.setup: Suggestive tape titles imply something inappropriate
jokes.punchline: All the 'dirty' footage is just messy pumpkin carving
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons misdirection - builds up expectation then deflates it perfectly

jokes.text: If I ever needed proof of the existence of God, here it is.
jokes.setup: Bart finds a tape labeled 'Bart Sad'
jokes.punchline: Bart considers his own misery as divine evidence
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that's very much in Bart's character - finding joy in his own pain

jokes.text: What? You never heard of flush dance?
jokes.setup: Perfect Strangers reference with Balki dancing in toilet
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'Flashdance' becoming 'flush dance'
jokes.explanation: Decent pun but relies heavily on pop culture reference without much additional wit

jokes.text: CHORUS [SINGING]: He's the baby Whose mouth smells like death Run for your life It's Baby Stink-Breath
jokes.setup: Commercial jingle format
jokes.punchline: Cheerful song about a baby with death breath
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd commercial parody - dark concept delivered as upbeat jingle

jokes.text: This isn't 'Bart Sad.' It's 'Bart's Ad.'
jokes.setup: Bart thought tape was about him being sad
jokes.punchline: Pun reveals it's actually his commercial
jokes.explanation: Clean wordplay that drives the plot forward while being genuinely clever

jokes.text: These soothing patches alter your baby's DNA... while leaving the RNA untouched.
jokes.setup: Commercial for baby breath product
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions altering DNA like it's no big deal
jokes.explanation: Dark satire of casual medical advertising - treats genetic modification as routine

jokes.text: I was going to tell you on my deathbed.
jokes.setup: Bart asks why parents never told him about commercial
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact deathbed revelation plan
jokes.explanation: Perfectly delivered Marge line - casual approach to major confession

jokes.text: La-Ia-Ia-Ia-Ia.
jokes.setup: Marge asking about college fund value
jokes.punchline: Homer's childish noise to avoid admitting he spent it all
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer avoidance tactic - childish but completely in character

jokes.text: I needed it. I had to buy back some incriminating photos.
jokes.setup: Homer admitting he spent Bart's money
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was for mysterious incriminating photos
jokes.explanation: Sets up visual gag - implies scandalous photos but...

jokes.text: I know this looks bad, but if you reverse it, Daddy's a hero.
jokes.setup: Homer showing 'incriminating' photos of baby Bart in danger
jokes.punchline: Claims playing it in reverse makes him heroic
jokes.explanation: Clever visual comedy concept - Homer's twisted logic about photo sequence

jokes.text: That's okay. His cute little hands can't even fit around my neck. He-- [YELLING] Now he's got it.
jokes.setup: Homer dismissing Bart's strangling attempt
jokes.punchline: Mid-sentence realization that Bart succeeded
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing - the confident dismissal interrupted by reality

jokes.text: Bart, the larynx is not a plaything.
jokes.setup: Bart strangling Homer
jokes.punchline: Marge's understated parenting correction
jokes.explanation: Classic Marge - treating violence like minor misbehavior with medical precision

jokes.text: When you're 17, I'm gonna know the end is near... so I'm gonna work you like a dog.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming ownership of Bart until 18
jokes.punchline: Plans to exploit him more as deadline approaches
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about parental exploitation - very Homer in its selfishness

jokes.text: When my mom wants to get back at my dad, she uses her lawyer. - Does it make him cry? - More than normal.
jokes.setup: Discussing revenge against parents
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of father's constant crying
jokes.explanation: Dark insight into Milhouse's dysfunctional family - 'more than normal' is perfectly delivered

jokes.text: Badger, Haggle and Bill... Luvum and Burnham, Family Law... Hackey, Joke and Dunnit
jokes.setup: Bart looking through law firm names
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous pun-based firm names
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay escalation but feels somewhat formulaic for The Simpsons

jokes.text: Good point, Dad. What would Jesus do? Jesus? I thought it was Geppetto. Aw, pfft.
jokes.setup: Bart looking at Homer's WWJD bracelet
jokes.punchline: Homer thought it was What Would Geppetto Do
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer confusion - religious symbol misunderstood in character-appropriate way

jokes.text: It comes with a side of bacon. - Is it crispy? - Yes. - But not too crispy? - No.
jokes.setup: Process server trying to get Homer to open door
jokes.punchline: Homer's detailed bacon preferences even in legal crisis
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Homer's food obsession overrides all other concerns

jokes.text: Don't you like being a dude?
jokes.setup: Bart wants to be emancipated
jokes.punchline: Homer confuses 'emancipated' with 'emasculated'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer malapropism - confusion between similar-sounding legal terms

jokes.text: I wanted a sewing room but not like this. Not like this.
jokes.setup: Learning Bart might move out
jokes.punchline: Marge's practical concern delivered dramatically
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge - dramatic delivery of mundane domestic concern

jokes.text: Hey, my dad was lousy and I didn't sue him. I just dumped him in the cheapest home I could find.
jokes.setup: Homer defending his parenting
jokes.punchline: Admits to elder abuse as positive example
jokes.explanation: Dark Homer logic - presents terrible behavior as restraint

jokes.text: Hey. My IV is empty. And my catheter is full.
jokes.setup: Cut to Grandpa in nursing home
jokes.punchline: Medical neglect delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Follow-through on nursing home joke - Grandpa's casual suffering

jokes.text: Here and here. Let the records show that he pulled out the pockets of the doll.
jokes.setup: Lawyer asks Bart to show where father took money using doll
jokes.punchline: Literal pocket-pulling demonstration
jokes.explanation: Solid visual gag with good courtroom formality contrast

jokes.text: Why, you little ... Uh, I'm sorry, judge. That's a rare lapse in my normally calm demeanor.
jokes.setup: Judge mentions Homer's anger management problem
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately demonstrates it then claims it's rare
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic timing - Homer proves the point while denying it

jokes.text: Why, you little ... Why, you little ... Why, you little ...
jokes.setup: Stenographer reading back Homer's outburst
jokes.punchline: Homer keeps interrupting with same outburst
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Homer can't help proving his anger problem repeatedly

jokes.text: Whoo-hoo! You're still mine. And you thought I was a bad dad before ... --except in this case.
jokes.setup: Homer celebrates thinking he won
jokes.punchline: Judge's exception completely reverses the ruling
jokes.explanation: Perfect legal comedy timing - the exception reverses everything mid-celebration

jokes.text: That boy's about as safe living with you as a crawdad in a gumbo shack.
jokes.setup: Judge explaining ruling
jokes.punchline: Colorful Southern metaphor for danger
jokes.explanation: Colorful regional metaphor that's vivid and specific

jokes.text: Half goes to Bart, half goes to my Vegas wife. What's left for Moe?
jokes.setup: Judge garnishing Homer's wages
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual mention of Vegas wife and Moe debt
jokes.explanation: Reveals Homer's multiple financial obligations casually - typical Homer chaos

jokes.text: I was told this would be televised!
jokes.setup: Homer making courtroom scene
jokes.punchline: His main concern is TV coverage
jokes.explanation: Homer's misplaced priorities - wants fame even from family dissolution

jokes.text: Do you even know what a loft is? - No. I assume it has hay.
jokes.setup: Bart mentions renting a loft
jokes.punchline: Bart confuses 'loft' with 'hayloft'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Bart's childish understanding of adult concepts

jokes.text: Here's something to remember me by. - Ow! - Indian burn. - Look at it. Aw, that's so sweet. If I did it right, it's permanent.
jokes.setup: Lisa giving Bart something to remember her
jokes.punchline: It's a painful Indian burn she hopes is permanent
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling dynamic - affection expressed through minor violence

jokes.text: I'll let you swear in the house. Everything but the big three.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to convince Bart to stay
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to mysterious 'big three' swear words
jokes.explanation: Good mystery comedy - what are the 'big three' that even Homer won't allow?

jokes.text: Either give me some of that or let's get going.
jokes.setup: Emotional goodbye between Marge and Bart
jokes.punchline: Taxi driver's impatient interruption
jokes.explanation: Good timing to break emotional moment with practical concern

jokes.text: That's right. Crawling on your knees. [GRUNTING] Crawling!
jokes.setup: Homer predicting Bart will come crawling back
jokes.punchline: Homer literally demonstrates crawling
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment - Homer can't resist demonstrating his point literally

jokes.text: MAN: Be quiet in there! Some of us are trying to sell drugs!
jokes.setup: Bart making noise in his new apartment
jokes.punchline: Neighbor complaining about noise interfering with drug dealing
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - expects normal noise complaint, gets casual drug dealing admission

jokes.text: Attaboy, we just won the World Series. You and me together, yeah. [LAUGHS] [SCREAMS] He should've done that with the real Bart when he had the chance.
jokes.setup: Homer celebrating with stuffed animal replacement for Bart
jokes.punchline: Lisa's dark observation about missed bonding opportunities
jokes.explanation: Dark truth delivered by Lisa - Homer's better with replacement than real son

jokes.text: Boy, I wish I was a free man. Mom. Where's Puppy Goo Goo?
jokes.setup: Milhouse envying Bart's freedom
jokes.punchline: Immediately calls for mom and stuffed animal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse moment - wants independence but needs security blanket

jokes.text: Now, calm down. I'm perfectly safe. Murder's illegal in this state.
jokes.setup: Bart alone in scary apartment at night
jokes.punchline: His naive reassurance about legal protections
jokes.explanation: Good child logic - thinking laws provide actual protection from danger

jokes.text: Oh, wait. That's my old phone number. [SNAPS]
jokes.setup: Tony Hawk checking his business card
jokes.punchline: Casual attitude about outdated contact info
jokes.explanation: Minor observational humor - relatable celebrity moment but not particularly strong

jokes.text: Hey, Blink-182. - We have names. - Whatever. You can crank it up.
jokes.setup: Tony Hawk addressing the band
jokes.punchline: Dismissive attitude toward band members' individual identities
jokes.explanation: Celebrity dynamics humor - treating band as brand rather than individuals

jokes.text: Of all the sites on all the web, I had to click onto his.
jokes.setup: Homer discovering he can see Bart via webcam
jokes.punchline: Casablanca reference adapted to internet age
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture adaptation - classic movie line updated for web era

jokes.text: Ha, ha, did you see that? Your mom thinks you're still here.
jokes.setup: Marge pouring juice for absent Bart
jokes.punchline: Homer's oblivious mockery of Marge's grief ritual
jokes.explanation: Homer's insensitivity to Marge's coping mechanism - character-appropriate but cruel

jokes.text: It's taught me how to love and care for a child. [COOS] What the...? Where the hell is my sugar?
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his parenting improvement with sugar baby
jokes.punchline: Immediate failure when sugar baby disappears
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff - Homer's parenting exercise immediately fails

jokes.text: Condoleezza Marie ain't too playful tonight. And I don't remember her being this granulated.
jokes.setup: Moe treating bag of sugar as baby
jokes.punchline: Noticing sugar's texture as personality trait
jokes.explanation: Absurd continuation of sugar baby concept - Moe's genuine concern for granules

jokes.text: Cool guy, Tony Hawk. - Bart, you know Tony Hawk? Please, I'm trying to keep it quiet. Catch you later, Tony Hawk. Stay cool, Brett.
jokes.setup: Bart claiming he wants to keep Tony Hawk connection quiet
jokes.punchline: Uses Tony's full name in every sentence, Tony gets Bart's name wrong
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Bart name-drops while claiming discretion, Tony doesn't remember him

jokes.text: Here's $5. Buy yourself a suit and get busy. I'll buy a suit... of drugs! [LAUGHING HYSTERICALLY]
jokes.setup: Drug dealer giving Bart money and advice
jokes.punchline: 'Suit of drugs' wordplay with maniacal laughter
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay but feels somewhat random - drug dealer's enthusiastic punnery

jokes.text: And for every syringe I find, I get a dollar.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining his income source
jokes.punchline: Casual child labor involving drug paraphernalia
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Bart's dangerous living situation delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Don't worry. They're clip-ons.
jokes.setup: Homer horrified at mention of nipple piercing
jokes.punchline: Bart's innocent reassurance about fake piercings
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Bart's innocent solution to adult concern

jokes.text: Behold the delightful unicorn I got at the face painting pavilion. Dude, that was a tattoo parlor.
jokes.setup: Ned showing off face painting
jokes.punchline: Reveals he accidentally got a real tattoo
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned moment - his innocence leads to permanent mistake

jokes.text: An extreme word? Sure. [SHOUTING] My son is Bart Simpson and--! I didn't say extreme to the max.
jokes.setup: Tony asks for an 'extreme' word
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation vs Tony's clarification
jokes.explanation: Good misunderstanding between Homer's literal mind and skater slang

jokes.text: Yeah, I make up words too.
jokes.setup: Tony Hawk using skateboarding terminology
jokes.punchline: Homer misunderstanding professional jargon as made-up words
jokes.explanation: Good Homer confusion about specialized vocabulary, shows his outsider status

jokes.text: I'm challenging you, Hawk... in front of all these outcasts and dreamers... who can't even get into the Army, if you can believe it.
jokes.setup: Homer challenging Tony Hawk publicly
jokes.punchline: His assessment of the crowd as military rejects
jokes.explanation: Homer's typical middle-American prejudice against counterculture

jokes.text: I can't count how many times... your father's done something crazy like this. It's 300, Mom. I could've sworn it was 302.
jokes.setup: Marge saying she can't count Homer's crazy stunts
jokes.punchline: Lisa has exact count, they debate the number
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy - Lisa's precision vs Marge's figure of speech, casual debate about specific count

jokes.text: You know, I could save money by buying separate nuts... and mixing them at home.
jokes.setup: Homer on expensive skateboard performing well
jokes.punchline: His mind wanders to mundane money-saving strategies
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - distracted by practical concerns during extreme sports

jokes.text: This was never about being cool, it was about you not caring how I felt.
jokes.setup: Homer thinking winning contest will fix everything
jokes.punchline: Bart's emotional truth cuts through the spectacle
jokes.explanation: Emotional truth that grounds the episode - Bart's real issue isn't coolness

jokes.text: Oh, that's the dumbest thing I ever heard, you stupid little kid. Homer, you're heading for parental face-plant. Do a 180 emotional ollie.
jokes.setup: Homer immediately dismissing Bart's feelings
jokes.punchline: Tony translating advice into skateboard metaphors
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor translation - skateboard terms for emotional advice

jokes.text: Well, I am bald and important. Where do I sign?
jokes.setup: Commercial for 'bald, impotent men'
jokes.punchline: Homer mishears 'impotent' as 'important'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer mishearing - his ego makes him hear what he wants to hear

jokes.text: What did they say about my scalp? - Don't worry, Dad. it's a commercial. No one will remember this in 50 years.
jokes.setup: Homer concerned about side effects
jokes.punchline: Bart's reassurance about commercial longevity (ironic given we're watching it)
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about the permanence of recorded media - ironic given show's longevity
